zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Margaret Gifford於1535年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾分頓時，她的父親Sir Nicholas Gifford27歲，母親Lady Agnes Anne Masters25歲。她於1554年2月23日與Hugh Roger Sargent在英國諾薩普頓史爾庫廷哈爾結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1595年2月28日在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾分頓享年60歲，埋葬在英國諾薩普頓史爾庫廷哈爾聖 彼得 安德 聖 包利斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Margaret Gifford于1535年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾分頓时，她的父亲Sir Nicholas Gifford27岁，母亲Lady Agnes Anne Masters25岁。她于1554年2月23日与Hugh Roger Sargent在英国諾薩普頓史爾庫廷哈爾结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1595年2月28日在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾分頓享年60岁，埋葬在英国諾薩普頓史爾庫廷哈爾聖 彼得 安德 聖 包利斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Gifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Giffard, either from the Middle English personal name Gifard, derived from an Old French name of this form deriving from ancient Germanic geb- ‘give, gift’ + hard- ‘hardy, brave’, or perhaps sometimes a nickname from Old French gif(f)ard ‘chubby-cheeked, bloated’.

variants.name: Jeffords
meanings: English: variant of Gifford , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Margaret Gifford was born in 1535, in Finedon, Northamptonshire, England, her father, Sir Nicholas Gifford, was 27 and her mother, Lady Agnes Anne Masters, was 25. She married Hugh Roger Sargent on 23 February 1554, in Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 7 daughters. She died on 28 February 1595, in Finedon, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 60, and was buried in St Peter And St Paul's Church, Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Margaret Gifford est née en 1535, à Finedon, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Nicholas Gifford, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Lady Agnes Anne Masters, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Hugh Roger Sargent le 23 février 1554, à Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée le 28 février 1595, à Finedon, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterrée à St Peter And St Paul's Church, Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Margaret Gifford(이)가 1535년 영국 노르섬프토실르 핑네던에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Nicholas Gifford(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Agnes Anne Masters(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1554년 2월 23일 영국 노르섬프토실르 쿠르티홀에서 Hugh Roger Sargent(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1595년 2월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 핑네던에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르섬프토실르 쿠르티홀 스트 피터 앤드 스트 폴'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Hugh Roger Sargent em 23 de fevereiro de 1554, em Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 7 filhas. ru.St Peter And St Paul's Church, Courteenh: ст питир анд ст поул'с чурч, коуртинолл, нортамптоншир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Margaret Gifford родилась в 1535 г. в местности фаинедон, нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Nicholas Gifford было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Agnes Anne Masters, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh Roger Sargent 23 февраля 1554 г. в местности коуртинолл, нортамптоншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она умерла 28 февраля 1595 г. в местности фаинедон, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 60 лет и была похоронена в местности ст питир анд ст поул'с чурч, коуртинолл, нортамптоншир, Англия.

Se casó con Hugh Roger Sargent el 23 de febrero de 1554, en Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 28 de febrero de 1595, en Finedon, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultada en St Peter And St Paul's Church, Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.
